globalKeywords.description: Uses the computed value from the parent element.
Resets the property to inherited or initial depending on context.
Reverts to the value established by the user-agent stylesheet or previous cascade origin.
Reverts the property to the value from a previous cascade layer.

align-content.description: Uses the alignment behavior defined by the layout mode.
Aligns the item's baseline with the baseline of the alignment context.
Distributes items with equal space between them and no space at the ends.
Distributes items with equal space around each item.
Distributes items with equal space between and around items.
Stretches items to fill the available space along the alignment axis.
Places items at the center along the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the start edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the end edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the start edge in flex layout.
Aligns items to the end edge in flex layout.

align-items.description: Uses the alignment behavior defined by the layout mode.
Stretches items to fill the available space along the alignment axis.
Aligns the item's baseline with the baseline of the alignment context.
Places items at the center along the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the start edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the end edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns the item to its own start side based on writing mode and direction.
Aligns the item to its own end side based on writing mode and direction.
Aligns items to the start edge in flex layout.
Aligns items to the end edge in flex layout.

align-self.description: Uses the alignment behavior defined by the layout mode.
Stretches items to fill the available space along the alignment axis.
Aligns the item's baseline with the baseline of the alignment context.
Places items at the center along the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the start edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the end edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns the item to its own start side based on writing mode and direction.
Aligns the item to its own end side based on writing mode and direction.
Aligns items to the start edge in flex layout.
Aligns items to the end edge in flex layout.

align-tracks.description: Uses the alignment behavior defined by the layout mode.
Aligns the item's baseline with the baseline of the alignment context.
Distributes items with equal space between them and no space at the ends.
Distributes items with equal space around each item.
Distributes items with equal space between and around items.
Stretches items to fill the available space along the alignment axis.
Places items at the center along the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the start edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the end edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the start edge in flex layout.
Aligns items to the end edge in flex layout.

alignment-baseline.description: Aligns the baseline of the box with the baseline of the parent.
Aligns based on the alphabetic baseline used in Latin scripts.
Aligns based on the ideographic baseline used in East Asian scripts.
Aligns at the mathematical center of the text.
Aligns based on the central baseline of the parent.
Aligns based on the mathematical text baseline.
Aligns the top of the text to the parent's text-before-edge baseline.
Aligns the bottom of the text to the parent's text-after-edge baseline.

animation-composition.description: Uses only the values from the animation with highest priority, replacing others.
Adds animated values to the underlying property value when supported.
Accumulates animated values over successive iterations when supported.

animation-direction.description: Plays the animation forward on each cycle.
Plays the animation backward on each cycle.
Alternates direction each cycle, playing forward then backward.
Alternates direction each cycle, playing backward then forward.

animation-fill-mode.description: The animation does not apply styles before it starts or after it ends.
Retains the final computed values of the animation after it completes.
Applies starting keyframe values before the animation begins.
Applies both forwards and backwards fill behavior.

animation-play-state.description: The animation is paused and retains its current computed values.

animation-timing-function.description: Progresses at a constant rate over time.
Starts slow, speeds up, then slows down toward the end.
Jumps to the end value at the start of the animation.
Jumps to the end value at the end of the animation.

appearance.description: Allows the user agent to apply platform-native styling.

background-attachment.description: The background scrolls along with the element's contents.
The background is fixed with regard to the viewport; it does not move when scrolled.
The background scrolls with the element's content and is clipped to the element's padding box.

background-clip.description: The background is painted within the content box.
The background is painted within the padding box; it is clipped at the padding edge.
The background is painted within the border box.
The background is clipped to the foreground text (for text backgrounds).

background-origin.description: The background positioning area is the content box.
The background positioning area is the padding box.
The background positioning area is the border box.

background-position.description: Position the background image at the left edge.
Position the background image at the center.
Position the background image at the right edge.
Position the background image at the top edge.
Position the background image at the bottom edge.

background-repeat.description: Tile the background image horizontally only.
Tile the background image vertically only.
Tile the background image both horizontally and vertically.
Tile the image with leftover space distributed between tiles.
Tile the image and scale tiles to fill the area without clipping.
Do not tile the background image; draw it once.

background-size.description: Use the image's intrinsic size; do not scale unless necessary.
Scale the image to cover the entire background area; the image may be clipped.
Scale the image to fit within the background area while preserving aspect ratio.

border-bottom-style.description: A border consisting of circular dots.
A border consisting of short, evenly spaced line segments.
Two parallel lines with space between them.
A border that appears carved or recessed, based on shading.
A border that appears raised, the opposite of groove.
A border that gives the appearance of the element being sunken into the page.
A border that gives the appearance of the element being raised out of the page.

border-bottom-width.description: Uses a thin border width defined by the user agent.
Uses the default medium border width defined by the user agent.
Uses a thick border width defined by the user agent.

border-left-style.description: A border consisting of circular dots.
A border consisting of short, evenly spaced line segments.
Two parallel lines with space between them.
A border that appears carved or recessed, based on shading.
A border that appears raised, the opposite of groove.
A border that gives the appearance of the element being sunken into the page.
A border that gives the appearance of the element being raised out of the page.

border-left-width.description: Uses a thin border width defined by the user agent.
Uses the default medium border width defined by the user agent.
Uses a thick border width defined by the user agent.

border-right-style.description: A border consisting of circular dots.
A border consisting of short, evenly spaced line segments.
Two parallel lines with space between them.
A border that appears carved or recessed, based on shading.
A border that appears raised, the opposite of groove.
A border that gives the appearance of the element being sunken into the page.
A border that gives the appearance of the element being raised out of the page.

border-right-width.description: Uses a thin border width defined by the user agent.
Uses the default medium border width defined by the user agent.
Uses a thick border width defined by the user agent.

border-style.description: A border consisting of circular dots.
A border consisting of short, evenly spaced line segments.
Two parallel lines with space between them.
A border that appears carved or recessed, based on shading.
A border that appears raised, the opposite of groove.
A border that gives the appearance of the element being sunken into the page.
A border that gives the appearance of the element being raised out of the page.

border-top-style.description: A border consisting of circular dots.
A border consisting of short, evenly spaced line segments.
Two parallel lines with space between them.
A border that appears carved or recessed, based on shading.
A border that appears raised, the opposite of groove.
A border that gives the appearance of the element being sunken into the page.
A border that gives the appearance of the element being raised out of the page.

border-top-width.description: Uses a thin border width defined by the user agent.
Uses the default medium border width defined by the user agent.
Uses a thick border width defined by the user agent.

border-width.description: Uses a thin border width defined by the user agent.
Uses the default medium border width defined by the user agent.
Uses a thick border width defined by the user agent.

bottom.description: No offset is applied; layout determines final position.

box-sizing.description: Width and height apply only to the content box; padding and border are added outside.
Width and height include padding and border.

clear.description: Allows the element to be placed alongside floated elements if space allows.
Prevents the element from being placed alongside left-floated elements; it is moved below them.
Prevents the element from being placed alongside right-floated elements; it is moved below them.
Prevents the element from being placed alongside any floated elements; it is moved below all floats.
Prevents placement alongside floated elements on the inline-start side.
Prevents placement alongside floated elements on the inline-end side.

clip-path.description: Uses the content box as the clipping region.
Uses the padding box as the clipping region.
Uses the border box as the clipping region.
Uses the margin box as the clipping region.
Uses the object's fill bounding box as the clipping region.
Uses the object's stroke bounding box as the clipping region.
Uses the SVG viewBox as the clipping region.

color-scheme.description: No preference is indicated; the UA may choose any color scheme.
Indicates support for a light color scheme.
Indicates support for a dark color scheme.
Indicates that only the specified color scheme should be used.

cursor.description: Allows the UA to choose the cursor based on context.
Indicates an operation is in progress but interaction is possible.
Indicates the element can be moved.
Indicates the requested action will not be carried out.
Indicates content is currently being grabbed.

display.description: Behaves as a block-level element in block layout, typically starting on a new line and expanding to fill the available inline space.
Behaves as an inline-level element within text lines, without forcing line breaks.
Creates a container using the flex layout model for arranging its children along a main axis.
Removes the element from layout and interaction; it does not occupy space.
Behaves as inline or block depending on surrounding context. Support is limited.
Uses block and inline layout rules depending on context in the normal flow of the document.
Creates a new block formatting boundary, isolating internal layout from surrounding elements.
Behaves similarly to a block-level table element and uses table layout rules for its children.
Creates a container using the grid layout model, arranging children in rows and columns.
Uses the ruby layout model for East Asian annotation text.
Behaves as a block-level element and includes a marker, similar to an HTML list item.
The element itself does not participate in layout; only its children are rendered.
Behaves similarly to an inline-level table element and uses table layout rules for its children.
Creates an inline-level container that arranges its children using the flex layout model.
Creates an inline-level container that arranges its children using the grid layout model.

flex.description: Disables flex sizing; the item is sized according to its base size and does not grow or shrink.

flex-basis.description: The item's base size is determined solely by its content.

flex-direction.description: Sets the main axis to the inline direction, placing items horizontally in writing order.
Sets the main axis to the inline direction, placing items horizontally in reverse writing order.
Sets the main axis to the block direction, placing items vertically in block progression order.
Sets the main axis to the block direction, placing items vertically in reverse block progression order.

flex-wrap.description: Prevents items from wrapping; all items are placed on a single line along the main axis.
Allows items to wrap onto multiple lines, progressing in block direction.
Allows items to wrap onto multiple lines, but in the reverse block direction.

float.description: Positions the element on the left side of its containing block and allows inline content to wrap around it.
Positions the element on the right side of its containing block and allows inline content to wrap around it.
Does not float; the element remains in normal block flow.
Floats the element to the inline-start side, depending on writing direction.
Floats the element to the inline-end side, depending on writing direction.

font-family.description: Uses a serif typeface with small finishing strokes.
Uses a sans-serif typeface with no finishing strokes.
Uses the default user-interface font of the operating system.
Uses a typeface that mimics handwriting.
Uses a decorative or playful typeface.
Uses a typeface designed for mathematical typesetting.
Uses a typeface where all characters have equal width.
Uses the system UI serif typeface.
Uses the system UI sans-serif typeface.
Uses the system UI monospace typeface.
Uses the system UI rounded typeface.
Uses a typeface designed to display emoji.
Uses a Chinese fangsong style typeface.

font-kerning.description: Applies kerning based on typeface and UA heuristics.
Enables kerning when provided by the typeface.

font-optical-sizing.description: Adjusts glyph shapes to improve readability at the current font size.

font-size.description: Uses the smallest predefined font size.
Uses a very small predefined font size.
Uses a small predefined font size.
Uses the default medium font size.
Uses a large predefined font size.
Uses a very large predefined font size.
Uses the largest predefined font size.
Uses a font size larger than the parent element.
Uses a font size smaller than the parent element.

font-smooth.description: Allows the browser or operating system to determine text smoothing behavior.
Disables font anti-aliasing, rendering text with sharp pixel edges.
Forces anti-aliasing regardless of system or browser settings.

font-stretch.description: Uses a narrower typeface width if available.
Uses a wider typeface width if available.

font-style.description: Uses the typeface's standard upright design.
Uses the typeface's italic design, where available, to represent emphasis or alternate form.
Applies a slanted version of the upright typeface when a true italic design is not used.

font-variant.description: Uses the default glyph substitution behavior.
Replaces lowercase letters with small capital glyphs when available.

font-weight.description: Uses the typeface's standard weight (typically equivalent to 400).
Uses a heavier weight than the normal weight (typically equivalent to 700).
Uses a weight heavier than the inherited weight based on typeface weight mappings.
Uses a weight lighter than the inherited weight based on typeface weight mappings.

grid-auto-columns.description: Implicit columns size themselves based on content and layout constraints.

grid-auto-flow.description: Places items by filling each row before moving to the next.
Places items by filling each column before moving to the next.
Allows backfilling earlier gaps in the grid if smaller items can fit.

grid-auto-rows.description: Implicit rows size themselves based on content and layout constraints.

grid-column.description: The item is placed according to auto-placement rules along the column axis.
Extends the item across a specified number of column tracks.

grid-column-end.description: The item's column end line is determined by auto-placement or other layout constraints.
Extends the item across a specified number of column tracks from the start line.

grid-column-start.description: The item's column start line is determined by auto-placement or other layout constraints.
Extends the item across a specified number of column tracks from this start position.

grid-row.description: The item is placed according to auto-placement rules along the row axis.
Extends the item across a specified number of row tracks.

grid-row-end.description: The item's row end line is determined by auto-placement or other layout constraints.
Extends the item across a specified number of row tracks from the start line.

grid-row-start.description: The item's row start line is determined by auto-placement or other layout constraints.
Extends the item across a specified number of row tracks from this start position.

grid-template-areas.description: No named grid areas are defined.

grid-template-columns.description: No explicit column tracks are defined; columns are created only as needed.

grid-template-rows.description: No explicit row tracks are defined; rows are created only as needed.

height.description: Determines the size based on content and layout context.
Sizes the element based on its smallest possible content size without overflow.
Sizes the element based on its largest unbreakable content.
Clamps the size between the min-content and max-content sizes.

hyphens.description: Prevents words from being hyphenated automatically.
Allows hyphenation only where explicit hyphens exist in the text.
Allows the browser to insert hyphens at appropriate break points.

image-rendering.description: Uses the default image scaling algorithm.
Preserves sharp edges when scaling, using pixel-aligned rendering.
Scales images by enlarging individual pixels.

justify-content.description: Uses the alignment behavior defined by the layout mode.
Distributes items with equal space between them and no space at the ends.
Distributes items with equal space around each item.
Distributes items with equal space between and around items.
Stretches items to fill the available space along the alignment axis.
Places items at the center along the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the start edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the end edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the start edge in flex layout.
Aligns items to the end edge in flex layout.
Aligns items to the left edge in horizontal writing modes.
Aligns items to the right edge in horizontal writing modes.

justify-items.description: Uses the alignment behavior defined by the layout mode.
Stretches items to fill the available space along the alignment axis.
Aligns the item's baseline with the baseline of the alignment context.
Places items at the center along the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the start edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the end edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns the item to its own start side based on writing mode and direction.
Aligns the item to its own end side based on writing mode and direction.
Aligns items to the start edge in flex layout.
Aligns items to the end edge in flex layout.
Aligns items to the left edge in horizontal writing modes.
Aligns items to the right edge in horizontal writing modes.

justify-self.description: Uses the alignment behavior defined by the layout mode.
Stretches items to fill the available space along the alignment axis.
Aligns the item's baseline with the baseline of the alignment context.
Places items at the center along the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the start edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the end edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns the item to its own start side based on writing mode and direction.
Aligns the item to its own end side based on writing mode and direction.
Aligns items to the start edge in flex layout.
Aligns items to the end edge in flex layout.
Aligns items to the left edge in horizontal writing modes.
Aligns items to the right edge in horizontal writing modes.

justify-tracks.description: Uses the alignment behavior defined by the layout mode.
Distributes items with equal space between them and no space at the ends.
Distributes items with equal space around each item.
Distributes items with equal space between and around items.
Stretches items to fill the available space along the alignment axis.
Places items at the center along the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the start edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the end edge of the alignment axis.
Aligns items to the start edge in flex layout.
Aligns items to the end edge in flex layout.
Aligns items to the left edge in horizontal writing modes.
Aligns items to the right edge in horizontal writing modes.

left.description: No offset is applied; layout determines final position.

letter-spacing.description: Uses the default spacing between letters based on the typeface.

line-break.description: Allows the browser to choose the line breaking strategy based on language and context.
Applies a looser line breaking strategy, allowing more break opportunities.
Uses the default line breaking rules.
Applies a stricter line breaking strategy, reducing break opportunities.
Allows breaks at arbitrary points to prevent overflow.

line-height.description: Sets the line height to a UA-defined default based on the font size.

list-style-image.description: No image is used as the list marker.

list-style-position.description: The marker is placed inside the content box.
The marker is placed outside the content box.

margin-bottom.description: Let the user agent calculate the margin; commonly used for centering block elements horizontally.

margin-left.description: Let the user agent calculate the margin; commonly used for centering block elements horizontally.

margin-right.description: Let the user agent calculate the margin; commonly used for centering block elements horizontally.

margin-top.description: Let the user agent calculate the margin; commonly used for centering block elements horizontally.

mask-clip.description: Clips the mask to the content box.
Clips the mask to the padding box.
Clips the mask to the border box.
Clips the mask to the SVG viewBox.

mask-mode.description: Interprets the mask using the alpha channel.
Interprets the mask using luminance values.
Uses the mask mode that matches the source image type.

mask-origin.description: Positions the mask relative to the content box.
Positions the mask relative to the padding box.
Positions the mask relative to the border box.

mask-type.description: Uses luminance to determine mask transparency.
Uses alpha values to determine mask transparency.

max-height.description: Sizes the element based on its smallest possible content size without overflow.
Sizes the element based on its largest unbreakable content.
Clamps the size between the min-content and max-content sizes.

max-width.description: Sizes the element based on its smallest possible content size without overflow.
Sizes the element based on its largest unbreakable content.
Clamps the size between the min-content and max-content sizes.

min-height.description: Determines the size based on content and layout context.
Sizes the element based on its smallest possible content size without overflow.
Sizes the element based on its largest unbreakable content.
Clamps the size between the min-content and max-content sizes.

min-width.description: Determines the size based on content and layout context.
Sizes the element based on its smallest possible content size without overflow.
Sizes the element based on its largest unbreakable content.
Clamps the size between the min-content and max-content sizes.

mix-blend-mode.description: No blending; the element is drawn normally.
Multiplies backdrop and source colors, resulting in darker colors.
Inverts, multiplies, and inverts again; results in a lighter effect.
Combines multiply and screen modes to preserve highlights and shadows.
Selects the darker of backdrop and source colors.
Selects the lighter of backdrop and source colors.
Brightens the backdrop to reflect the source.
Darkens the backdrop to reflect the source.
Multiplies or screens colors depending on source lightness.
Applies a subtle lighting effect often used for shading.
Subtracts darker colors from lighter ones to create contrast.
Similar to difference but with lower contrast.
Preserves luminance and saturation of backdrop while adopting hue of source.
Preserves luminance and hue of backdrop while adopting saturation of source.
Preserves luminance while adopting hue and saturation of source.
Preserves hue and saturation while adopting luminance of source.
Lightens the backdrop by adding color values from the source.

object-fit.description: Stretches the replaced element to fill the content box, ignoring aspect ratio.
Scales the replaced element to fit within the content box while preserving aspect ratio.
Scales the replaced element to cover the content box while preserving aspect ratio; parts may be clipped.
Preserves intrinsic size; no scaling applied.
Behaves as either none or contain, whichever results in a smaller object size.

outline-style.description: Allows the user agent to determine an appropriate outline style.
Draws a dotted line as the outline.
Draws a dashed line as the outline.
Draws a single continuous line as the outline.
Draws two parallel lines as the outline.
Draws the outline as if embedded into the surface.
Draws the outline as if coming out of the surface.

outline-width.description: Uses the default medium outline width.

overflow.description: Content is not clipped and may render outside the element's box in the normal flow.
Content that overflows the element's box is clipped and not scrollable.
Content is clipped to the element's box without providing scroll functionality.
Content is clipped to the element's box and scrollbars are always shown, even if not needed.
Content is clipped to the element's box and scrollbars are shown only if necessary.

overflow-anchor.description: Scroll anchoring is enabled to reduce visible layout shifts.
Disables scroll anchoring for the element.

overflow-block.description: Allows overflow to extend beyond the block axis boundary.
Clips overflow content along the block axis.
Provides scrolling along the block axis even if unnecessary.
Allows scrolling along the block axis when needed.

overflow-clip-box.description: Overflow clipping uses the padding box as the boundary.
Overflow clipping uses the content box as the boundary.

overflow-clip-margin.description: Overflow clipping margin is measured from the content box.
Overflow clipping margin is measured from the padding box.
Overflow clipping margin is measured from the border box.

overflow-inline.description: Allows overflow to extend beyond the inline axis boundary.
Clips overflow content along the inline axis.
Provides scrolling along the inline axis even if unnecessary.
Allows scrolling along the inline axis when needed.

overflow-wrap.description: Lines break only at allowed break points.
Allows unbreakable words to be broken to prevent overflow.
Lines may break at any point if necessary.

overflow-x.description: Content is not clipped and may render outside the element's box in the normal flow.
Content that overflows the element's box is clipped and not scrollable.
Content is clipped to the element's box without providing scroll functionality.
Content is clipped to the element's box and scrollbars are always shown, even if not needed.
Content is clipped to the element's box and scrollbars are shown only if necessary.

overflow-y.description: Content is not clipped and may render outside the element's box in the normal flow.
Content that overflows the element's box is clipped and not scrollable.
Content is clipped to the element's box without providing scroll functionality.
Content is clipped to the element's box and scrollbars are always shown, even if not needed.
Content is clipped to the element's box and scrollbars are shown only if necessary.

paint-order.description: Uses the default rendering order: fill, then stroke, then markers.
Renders fill before stroke and markers.
Renders stroke before fill and markers.
Renders markers before fill and stroke.

position.description: Element participates in normal flow without offset positioning.
Element remains in normal flow but may be shifted relative to its normal position using inset properties.
Element is removed from normal flow and positioned relative to its nearest positioned ancestor or the initial containing block.
Element behaves as relative until a scroll threshold is passed, then behaves as fixed.
Element is removed from normal flow and positioned relative to the viewport.

right.description: No offset is applied; layout determines final position.

stroke-linecap.description: Ends the stroke exactly at the endpoint with no extension.
Adds a semicircular end to the stroke extending beyond the endpoint.
Adds a square end to the stroke extending beyond the endpoint.

stroke-linejoin.description: Joins stroke segments with a sharp corner extended to a miter point.
Joins stroke segments with a rounded corner.
Joins stroke segments with a flattened corner.

text-align.description: Aligns text to the start of the inline axis.
Aligns text to the end of the inline axis.
Aligns text to the left side of the line box.
Aligns text to the right side of the line box.
Aligns text to the center of the line box.
Expands spacing between inline items so lines align along both start and end edges.

text-decoration-line.description: No text decoration line is drawn.
Draws a line beneath the text.
Draws a line above the text.
Draws a line through the text.

text-decoration-thickness.description: Determines thickness automatically based on font metrics.
Uses the thickness specified in the font's metrics when available.

text-justify.description: Allows the browser to choose the justification algorithm.
Adjusts spacing between characters to justify text.
Adjusts spacing between words to justify text.

text-orientation.description: Combines upright and rotated glyphs depending on character type.
Displays all characters upright, without rotation.
Displays characters rotated as if rotated sideways.

text-overflow.description: Truncates text at the content edge without adding an indicator.
Displays an ellipsis (…) to indicate clipped text.

text-rendering.description: Lets the browser choose an appropriate text rendering strategy.
Prioritizes legibility, enabling advanced glyph shaping when available.
Prioritizes precise glyph geometry over speed.

text-size-adjust.description: Allows the browser to adjust text size for readability.

text-transform.description: Transforms the first character of each word to uppercase.
Transforms characters to their full-width forms.
Transforms small kana characters to their full-size forms.

top.description: No offset is applied; layout determines final position.

transform.description: Applies no transform; element remains in its original position and size.

transform-origin.description: Sets the origin for transforms to the left edge of the element.
Sets the origin for transforms to the center of the element.
Sets the origin for transforms to the right edge of the element.
Sets the origin for transforms to the top edge of the element.
Sets the origin for transforms to the bottom edge of the element.

transition-timing-function.description: Starts slow, speeds up, then slows down toward the end (cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1)).
Jumps to the end value at the beginning of the transition (a single step).
Remains at the start value until the end of the transition (a single step).

vector-effect.description: The stroke scales and transforms normally with the element.
The stroke width remains constant even when the element is scaled.
The entire element maintains constant sizing during scaling transforms.
Prevents rotation effects from being applied during transforms.
Renders the element as if fixed to the screen coordinate system.

vertical-align.description: Aligns the baseline of the element with the baseline of its parent.
Aligns the element as subscript text below the baseline.
Aligns the element as superscript text above the baseline.
Aligns the top of the element with the top of the parent's font metrics box.
Aligns the bottom of the element with the bottom of the parent's font metrics box.
Aligns the midpoint of the element with half the x-height above the parent's baseline.
Aligns the top of the element with the top of the tallest element on the line.
Aligns the bottom of the element with the bottom of the lowest element on the line.

visibility.description: The element is visible and participates in layout and rendering.
The element is invisible but still occupies layout space.
For table elements, removes the row/column and frees the space; otherwise behaves like hidden.

white-space.description: Collapses whitespace and wraps lines as needed.
Preserves whitespace and prevents line wrapping.
Preserves whitespace and wraps lines as needed.
Collapses sequences of spaces but preserves line breaks, wrapping when necessary.

width.description: Determines the size based on content and layout context.
Sizes the element based on its smallest possible content size without overflow.
Sizes the element based on its largest unbreakable content.
Clamps the size between the min-content and max-content sizes.

word-break.description: Uses standard line break rules based on the writing system.
Allows line breaks between any characters to prevent overflow.
Prevents breaking within words in CJK text, keeping characters grouped together.

word-spacing.description: Uses the default spacing between words based on the typeface.

word-wrap.description: Lines break only at allowed break points.
Allows unbreakable words to be broken to prevent overflow.

writing-mode.description: Text is laid out horizontally, progressing from top to bottom.
Text is laid out vertically, progressing from right to left.
Text is laid out vertically, progressing from left to right.

z-index.description: Element participates in the stacking context determined by its parent and painting order.
